  list-files, files        (Default Verb) List Files

bls 1.2.0
Copyright (c) 2024 Marc Behnke, All Rights Reserved

  -t, --target-path               Required. Target folder to copy files to

  -r, --replace-files             (Default: false) Toggle replacing files that
                                  already exist

  -f, --use-framework-globber     (Default: false) Revert to DotNet Framework
                                  Globber

  -b, --base-path                 Base path for globbing. Default is the current
                                  working directory.

  -x, --exclude                   Exclude File Specs. (1 or more) - Glob
                                  Formatting (e.g. **, *, ?)

  -q, --fully-qualified-paths     Output fully qualified paths

  -c, --case-sensitive            (Default: false) Toggle to add case sensitive
                                  path matching

  -p, --allow-duplicates          (Default: false) Toggle allowing duplicates if
                                  multiple base paths for faster output

  -a, --abort-on-access-errors    (Default: false) Toggle abort on file system
                                  access errors

  --help                          Display this help screen.

  --version                       Display version information.

  Include File Specs. (pos. 0)    Required. Initial positional args (1 or more)
                                  - Glob Formatting (e.g. **, *, ?)

  -b, --base-paths                One or more base paths for globbing. Default
                                  is the current working directory.

  -f, --use-framework-globber     (Default: false) Revert to DotNet Framework
                                  Globber

  -s, --sort                      Sort by (N)ame, (E)xtension, (D)ate, (S)ize.
                                  ex: -sd  ex: -s date

  -d, --descending                Sort in descending order. ex: -dsd  ex: -s
                                  date -d

  -t, --details                   (Default: false) Display Attributes, Last
                                  Write Time, and File Size

  -w, --owner                     (Default: false) Display Owner (Windows Only)

                                  Formatting (e.g. **, *, ?)
